channel.title: Visual Studio Code on Tue Nguyen
channel.description: Recent content in Visual Studio Code on Tue Nguyen

item.title: Code formatting
item.description: Code formatting refers to ways of organizing and structuring your code. It is an essential aspect of software development with the ultimate goal to make your codebase more consistent, readable, and maintainable. Prettier is an excellent extension that makes code formatting in VS Code much simpler. Follow the steps below to install and config Prettier. Installation Go to Extensions (Ctrl + Shift + X), search for Prettier - Code formatter, and install it.

item.title: Cool features
item.description: Zen mode Zen mode is a distract-free mode that hides away unnecessary detail and allows you to focus just on the code. The zen mode can be toggled using Ctrl + K Z. You can also quickly exit the zen mode by double pressing Esc. This is the layout of the normal mode. And this is the layout of the zen mode. Minimap For a large file with many lines of code, you can quickly scroll up and down the file using the minimap on the right side of the editor.

item.title: Interface
item.description: The default layout of VS Code is divided into 6 main parts as follows. Menu Bar Activity Bar Side Bar Editor Panel Status Bar Menu Bar This is the narrow bar at the very top. It provides a GUI for essential operations grouped into different menus such as File, Edit, View, etc. However, most of those operations can be done very quickly with hotkeys. Activity Bar This is the narrow bar at the leftmost.

item.title: Introduction
item.description: What is VS Code? Visual Studio Code (or VS Code) is a free, open-source, lightweight, and cross-platform code editor developed by Microsoft based on the Electron Framework. It is one of the most popular code editors used and recommended by programmers at all levels, from beginners to experienced professionals. Features Here are some of the highlighted features that have made VS Code popular. Free. You pay nothing to use it. Lightweight.

item.title: Keyboard shortcuts
item.description: This post summarizes some of the most common keyboard shortcuts that can significantly boost your productivity while working with VS Code. In case you forget or want to search for a particular hotkey, you can press Ctrl + K Ctrl + S. It will show the key binding table together with a search box for you to type in the keywords. Note that the hotkeys introduced in this tutorial is intended for Windows and Linux users.

item.title: Markdown
item.description: Default support VS Code supports Markdown natively, meaning that it recognizes any file with .md extensions as a Markdown file and provides various supports such as syntax highlighting, outline, snippets, etc. Smart text editing VS Code supports many hotkeys that mimic those that you normally find when working with a rich text editor like Microsoft Word. Here are some examples. Make bold with Ctrl + B Make italic with Ctrl + I Auto-extend list items and block quotes when hitting Enter It also provides some convenient hotkeys.

item.title: Python
item.description: Install a Python interpreter You need a Python interpreter to run a Python program. Thus, make sure that Python is already installed on your machine. You can check this by running python --version on a terminal. Choose an interpreter Whenever you open a file with the extension .py (Python file) or .ipynb (Jupyter Notebook file), VS Code will immediately scan for a Python interpreter available on your machine so that you can execute the code.

item.title: Settings
item.description: Settings allow you to customize VS Code in various aspects so that you feel most comfortable and productive. User and workspace settings There are two types of settings: user and workspace. User settings apply globally for every project whenever you open VS Code. Workspace settings only apply to the project that is currently open. Open settings Settings can be accessed and modified either through GUI or through the JSON file. I prefer the latter although it seems intimidating at first.

item.title: Themes
item.description: You will spend a lot of time looking at the IDE while writing code. Thus, it is crucial to have a theme that is nice to the eyes. Fortunately, there are a lot of beautiful themes available on VS Code marketplace, and it is very easy to install and activate a new theme. Types of themes In VS Code, themes are categorized into 3 types as follows. Color themes. A color theme change both the GUI icons (e.
